slices.focus: Saturday clinic for pre-review document bottlenecks
slices.summary: Discovered through delay-category tracking that application files were being lost before formal review due to heirship documentation and deed-copy issues. Launched Saturday clinics with title company assistance and bilingual intake volunteers, finally unblocking the upstream bottleneck. First real progress in months. Plans to document concrete success cases and pitch the clinic as a pilot to secure stable title-access support.
open_loops: How to keep the human touch and informal trust when scaling the clinic model beyond the pilot
Whether more stable title company access and additional staffing can be secured through the pilot evidence
Risk of being seen as a reactive firefighter rather than a systems improver who addresses root causes
decisions: Document two to three concrete success cases and write a pilot summary to build the case upward
Frame the pitch to leadership around reducing pre-review attrition as the core metric
Standardize the clinic process scaffolding (intake, triage, follow-up) while keeping conversational flexibility for residents
